title: Module 4 handout. Context, sources, and checks.
subtitle: RAG, deep research, tokens, hallucinations, and neutral prompts in one worksheet.

coreTopics.title: 1. RAG: answer from sources
coreTopics.meaning: When you need facts, give AI documents, data, or official sources and ask it to cite what it uses.

coreTopics.title: 2. Deep research: split the question into stages
coreTopics.meaning: Turn a large topic into 2–4 smaller questions: search, compare, synthesize, and conclude.

coreTopics.title: 3. Tokens and context window
coreTopics.meaning: AI can only see a limited amount of text, so shorten long material, split it, and repeat the most important context.

coreTopics.title: 4. Hallucinations: check before you trust
coreTopics.meaning: If there is no source, date, or clear basis, treat the answer as a draft and verify it before use.

coreTopics.title: 5. Prompt manipulation: avoid leading questions
coreTopics.meaning: Do not ask in a way that only confirms your view; ask for arguments for, against, and what is unclear.

checklist: Does the answer include a source or clearly say that no source is available?
Is the date, period, or document version visible?
Did AI get enough context so it does not guess?
Is the prompt not leading toward one desired answer?
For long text, did you add a summary or clear sections?
starterPrompt:
META: You are my source and reliability checker.
INPUT: I will paste an AI answer or my prompt below: [paste text].
OUTPUT: Check 5 things: (1) whether there are sources, (2) whether the date / version is clear, (3) where there may be a guess, (4) whether the question is leading, (5) what to fix before using it at work.
QUALITY: If something cannot be checked, clearly write "I don't know" and suggest which source is needed.
footerText: Prompt Anatomy – prompt structure training. © Course material.
